_meta.title: BNS 2023 Traffic Offence Sections — Mapped to MVA 1988
_meta.description: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023 sections applicable to road/traffic offences, mapped to corresponding Motor Vehicles Act sections

mappings.bnsTitle: Rash driving or riding on a public way
Negligent conduct with respect to poisonous substance — road endangerment
mappings.mvaTitle: Driving by a drunken person or by a person under influence of drugs
mappings.notes: BNS Section 285 covers negligent acts with intoxicants; drunk driving prosecuted under both MVA 185 and BNS 285

mappings.mvaTitle: Duty of driver in case of accident and injury to a person
mappings.notes: IPC Section 304A equivalent. Hit-and-run cases can attract BNS 106 (culpable homicide by negligence). BNS 106(2) specifically covers hit-and-run with imprisonment up to 10 years.

mappings.bnsTitle: Act endangering life or personal safety of others
mappings.notes: IPC Section 336 equivalent. Applies to racing, stunts, or extremely reckless driving endangering others.

mappings.bnsTitle: Act causing hurt by endangering life
mappings.notes: IPC Section 337 equivalent. If dangerous driving causes hurt to any person.

mappings.bnsTitle: Act causing grievous hurt by endangering life
mappings.notes: IPC Section 338 equivalent. If dangerous driving causes grievous hurt.

mappings.bnsTitle: Causing hurt by act endangering life of others — road rage
mappings.notes: Road rage incidents involving physical assault can be charged under BNS 316 in addition to MVA offences.

mappings.bnsTitle: Causing grievous hurt by act endangering life — road rage
mappings.notes: Road rage causing grievous hurt. Applicable alongside MVA penalty.
Guardian/owner who allows juvenile to drive can be charged with abetment under BNS 109, alongside MVA 199A.

mappings.bnsTitle: Causing mischief — road blockade / vehicle damage
mappings.mvaTitle: Causing obstruction or mischief on public road
mappings.notes: IPC Section 425 equivalent. Blocking roads or damaging vehicles can attract BNS 329.

mappings.mvaTitle: Power to detain vehicles used without certificate of registration
mappings.notes: IPC Section 379 equivalent. Driving a stolen vehicle invokes BNS 303 and MVA violations.
IPC Section 506 equivalent. Threatening behaviour during road rage can attract BNS 351.
